meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Deploy Apps Like a Pro (Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment errors. Master Claude Code skills setup to launch your first student project with confidence using this expert guide.
content.answer_block: Deployment failure often stems from environment mismatches and unoptimized build configurations. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you automate the complex infrastructure setup, transforming local code into production-ready deployments. This workflow bridges the gap between your IDE and the cloud, ensuring your apps go live without manual configuration headaches.
content.intro:
You’ve spent days crafting the perfect logic, only to hit a wall of cryptic deployment logs and environment errors. That sinking feeling of 'it works on my machine' is the rite of passage every developer faces, but it shouldn't be the end of your project. The gap between a finished script and a live URL is where most student projects die, but it’s exactly where you can distinguish yourself as an engineer.

Today, we are changing the narrative. By mastering the Claude Code and Antigravity workflow, you gain the ability to orchestrate complex infrastructure as easily as writing a Python function. This isn't just about fixing a deployment error; it's about building the architectural confidence to launch whatever you can imagine.

steps.title: Environment Audit
steps.description: Before deploying, you must ensure your local environment matches production requirements. We use AI to scan for missing dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and identify any missing environment variables or dependencies that will cause a deployment failure on Vercel or Railway.

steps.title: Configuring the Build Pipeline
steps.description: Standardize your build process so the server knows exactly how to execute your code upon deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Dockerfile and a deployment configuration file that minimizes build time and optimizes memory usage.

steps.title: Automated Error Simulation
steps.description: Test your deployment script in a sandbox before pushing to production to catch runtime errors early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Simulate the build process for this project. Identify any potential race conditions or port binding issues that occur during initial startup.

steps.title: Infrastructure Provisioning
steps.description: Use Claude Code to interface with your cloud provider's CLI, automating the creation of your hosting instance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated deployment script that authenticates with my hosting provider and pushes the latest main branch.

steps.title: Post-Deployment Monitoring
steps.description: Set up a lightweight health check to ensure your app stays alive and responds to traffic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a health-check script that pings my production URL every 5 minutes and logs any 500-level errors to a text file.

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file to track required variables, or your deployment will fail silently on missing keys.
Implement a 'build-check' script in your package.json that runs unit tests before allowing a deployment command to execute.
Set your deployment target to 'staging' first; never push directly to production until you see a green status on the staging build.
Use Claude Code to document your deployment steps within a README.md file automatically, keeping your documentation in sync with your infrastructure.
Monitor cold-start times; if your app takes too long to load, use Claude to refactor the initial dependency imports.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on adding CI/CD pipelines to automate these steps every time you push code to GitHub.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student writing code; you are an engineer shipping products. Take this momentum and apply it to your portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
